basic_details.job_title: CNCI Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: Chittaranjan National Cancer Institute (CNCI) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 1 Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply by sending their application via Email. The last date to send the application email is 27-05-2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 28 years Age relaxation as per Govt. of India rules for SC/ST/OBC/PWD/Women.

qualification.education_qualification: MSc/MS in Chemistry (at least 55% or CGPA 7.0), National level eligibility test (CSIR NET/GATE etc.), BSc in Chemistry (honors), MSc/MS in Biochemistry or Bio-informatics or Molecular Biology or Biotechnology
qualification.details: At least 55% (or minimum CGPA of 7.0) in MSc/MS in Chemistry, with qualification in a national level eligibility test (CSIR NET, GATE etc.) along with BSc in Chemistry (honors) and masters' (M.Sc./MS) in Biochemistry or Bio-informatics Molecular Biology or Biotechnology.

how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 27-05-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CNCI JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position at CNCI is 27th May 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the JRF post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have an MSc/MS in Chemistry with at least 55% or CGPA 7.0, and have qualified in a national-level eligibility test like CSIR NET or GATE. A BSc in Chemistry (honors) and a Master's in Biochemistry, Bio-informatics, Molecular Biology, or Biotechnology are also required.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Junior Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) post is 28 years. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules for reserved categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the JRF role?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 37,000.00 per month, along with a 30% House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q5. How will candidates be selected for the JRF position?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting of eligible candidates followed by a walk-in interview or personal discussion.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee required for any category of candidates applying for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position at CNCI.

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for the CNCI JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: Interested candidates are required to apply by sending their application via email to the address specified in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are needed to be attached with the application?
faq.answer: You need to attach self-attested copies of all required documents, including educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.

faq.question: Q9. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is a total of 1 vacancy for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) post announced by Chittaranjan National Cancer Institute.

faq.question: Q10. Where is the Chittaranjan National Cancer Institute located?
faq.answer: Chittaranjan National Cancer Institute is located in Kolkata, West Bengal, with the pincode 700026.
